title: Internal storage systems for miniaturized intra-body controllable medical devices
abstract: A medical device includes a host structure that has an interior area and one or more internal storage systems that have one or more miniaturized compartments for housing one or more power supplies, energy storage devices, medications, imaging systems, computer processor controllers, communications transmitters and receivers, propulsion systems, therapy delivering devices (e.g., radiation sources), process waste, biopsies, blood and tissue samples, medical and surgical instruments, fluids, gases, powders and consumables.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A medical device for intra-body conveyance, the medical device comprising: a host structure defining an interior area and at least one internal storage system located at least partially in the interior area. 2. The medical device of claim 1, wherein the at least one internal storage system includes at least one miniaturized compartment. 3. The medical device of claim 1, wherein the host structure has a diameter of about 25mm and a length of about 75mm. 4. The medical device of claim 2, wherein the at least one miniaturized compartment is configured for housing at least one of a power supply, an energy storage device, a medication, an imaging system, a computer processor controller, a communications transmitters or receiver, a propulsion system, a therapy delivering device, a process waste, a biopsy sample, a blood or tissue samples, a medical or surgical instrument, a fluid, a gas, a powder, and a consumable material. 5. The medical device of claim 1, wherein the host structure includes at least one of a clinically inert material, a sterilizable material, an elastomeric material, a chemically reactive material, a chemically inert material, a disintegrable material, a dissolvable material, a collapsible material and a material having physical and chemical properties to withstand exposure to bodily fluids for a predetermined period of time. 6. A method for using the medical device of any one of the preceding claims, the method being directed to at least one of use in a gastro/intestinal tract, use in urology applications, use in a lung, use in a bladder, use in a nasal system, use in a reproductive system, use in performing Transurethral Resection of Bladder Tumors (TURBT), use in Transurethral Resection of the Prostate (TURP), use in trans rectal prostate ultrasound, biopsy, and radiation treatment. 7. A method for treating a patient, utilizing a medical device for intra-body conveyance of any of the preceding claims, the medical device comprising: a host structure defining an interior area and at least one internal storage system. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the at least one internal storage system comprises at least one miniaturized compartment. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the at least one miniaturized compartment is configured for housing at least one of a power supply, an energy storage device, a medication, an imaging system, a computer processor controller, a communications transmitters or receiver, a propulsion system, a therapy delivering device, a process waste, a biopsy sample, a blood or tissue samples, a medical or surgical instrument, a fluid, a gas, a powder, and a consumable material. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein at least one of the host structure and the internal storage systems comprises: at least one of a clinically inert material, a sterilizable material, an elastomeric material, a chemically reactive material, a chemically inert material, a disintegrable material, a dissolvable material, a collapsible material and a material having physical and chemical properties to withstand exposure to bodily fluids for a predetermined period of time.
